content.boundary: Applies to primary schools in settlements under 2,000 inhabitants where enrollment is near legal minimum thresholds; does not restore full municipal ownership or alter the KLIK legal-entity status of schools.
content.change_lever: Three-year reciprocal contracts between the state (via tankerületi center/county government) and municipal/inter-municipal associations, in which the state commits to maintaining a minimum staffing allowance for a defined period in exchange for municipal commitments to co-fund transport, shared facilities, or network reorganization.
content.name: Reciprocal rural-school contracts (convention-style) between state and municipalities
content.system_problem: Hungary's 2013 centralization eliminated municipal bargaining power and budget autonomy that most European closure-prevention mechanisms presume, meaning villages have no formal lever to negotiate staffing/teaching-post guarantees in exchange for local commitments to network reorganization.
